codeReview.summary: All three security fixes are comprehensively implemented and tested. PBKDF2 password hashing with 100k iterations and 16-byte random salts replaces the vulnerable SHA-256 implementation. Constant-time comparison via XOR-based comparison prevents timing attacks. R2 filename sanitization correctly blocks path traversal sequences (../, ..\), null bytes, absolute paths, and Windows drive letters. XSS prevention escapes all user-controlled values in subdomain listings including both subdomain names and share slugs. Legacy SHA-256 hashes are transparently migrated to PBKDF2 on first successful verification.

summary: Implementation passes review. All security requirements from VAL-FOUND-001, VAL-FOUND-003, and VAL-FOUND-004 are correctly implemented with comprehensive test coverage (14 security tests). The code follows established patterns, includes proper error codes (AUTH_FAILED, AUTH_REQUIRED, INVALID_FILENAME), and maintains backward compatibility through transparent legacy hash migration.
